events.title: ADAPPT 4.0
events.organizer: Mukesh Patel School of Technology Management and Engineering (NMIMS), Mumbai

raw.title: ADAPPT 4.0
raw.details:
Eligibility:

  • Open to inter-college and inter-specialization teams. 

Event Overview:

  • ADAPPT 4.0: The Startup Weekend is an overnight innovation challenge organized by IETE-SF MPSTME, designed to bridge the gap between industry challenges and entrepreneurial solutions. This unique event brings together students, industry experts, and technology to create real-world startup concepts that address pressing problems across four critical domains.
  • Unlike traditional hackathons or ideathons, ADAPPT 4.0 follows a Venture Sprint format where participants develop complete startup solutions, combining business strategy with technical implementation. Teams will hear directly from industry experts about actual challenges faced in their organizations, then spend 24 hours developing comprehensive solutions that include both a viable business model and a fully functional website.

Event Details:

  • Date: 1st - 2nd November 2025
  • Venue: MPSTME | NMIMS, Mumbai
  • Duration: 24 hours
  • Format: Overnight Venture Sprint

Event Format:

ADAPPT 4.0 follows the Venture Sprint methodology, which combines three essential elements of startup creation:

  • Phase 1: Problem Discovery: Industry experts from leading companies will present real-world challenges they face in their respective domains. These are not hypothetical problems but actual issues that organizations are struggling to solve. Participants will gain direct insight into industry pain points and market needs.
  • Phase 2: Business Development : Teams begin by developing a comprehensive business strategy for their solution. This includes:
    • Problem analysis and market research
    • Solution conceptualization and unique value proposition
    • Business model and revenue strategy
    • Target audience and go-to-market approach
    • Competitive analysis and differentiation
  • Phase 3: Technical Implementation: Following business planning, teams move into intensive technical development to create a fully functional website that demonstrates their solution. This website serves as the MVP (Minimum Viable Product) showcasing how the startup would operate in the real world.
  • Phase 4: Presentation and Evaluation: Teams present their complete venture, business strategy and technical product, to expert judges who evaluate both entrepreneurial viability and technical execution.

Domains

  • Domain 1: Digital Banking and Finance: Explore the dynamic world of financial technology where innovation meets security and accessibility. This domain focuses on transforming traditional banking systems through digital solutions that address payment processing, fraud detection, customer experience, and financial inclusion challenges faced by modern financial institutions.
  • Participants will tackle real-world problems in mobile banking security, seamless payment systems, digital lending platforms, cryptocurrency integration, wealth management automation, and creating accessible financial services for underserved populations. Solutions should combine cutting-edge technology with practical business viability and scalability.
  • Domain 2: Smart Crop Technologies: Dive into precision agriculture and sustainable farming technologies that empower farmers with data-driven decision making. This domain addresses critical challenges in modern agriculture including crop optimization, resource management, and connecting farmers to markets through innovative technological solutions.
  • Key focus areas include crop yield prediction and optimization, pest and disease detection systems, automated irrigation management, soil health monitoring, weather forecasting integration, farm-to-market supply chain solutions, and advisory platforms that make advanced agricultural technology accessible and affordable for farmers of all scales.
  • Domain 3: Smart Cities & Urban Tech: Transform urban living through intelligent infrastructure and sustainable city management solutions. This domain tackles the complex challenges of modern urbanization including waste management, energy efficiency, transportation, public safety, and creating livable, environmentally responsible cities for growing populations.
  • Participants will address problems in smart waste collection systems, traffic management and congestion reduction, energy-efficient infrastructure, water conservation technologies, air quality monitoring, public safety enhancements, digital governance platforms, intelligent parking solutions, and sustainable urban mobility that improve quality of life for city residents.
  • Domain 4: Cybersecurity and Data Protection: Navigate the critical landscape of digital security where protecting data and systems is paramount. This domain focuses on defending against evolving cyber threats while ensuring privacy, building trust, and maintaining data integrity in our increasingly connected world.
  • Challenge areas span threat detection and prevention systems, secure authentication mechanisms, data encryption technologies, privacy protection tools, vulnerability assessment platforms, security awareness solutions, cloud security implementations, IoT device protection, and compliance management systems that safeguard both organizations and individuals from sophisticated cyber attacks. 

Rules and Guidelines

General Rules 

  1. All team members must be present at registration and throughout the event
  2. Teams must work only on their assigned domain
  3. Teams cannot change members after registration closes
  4. All work must be original and created during the event timeline
  5. Use of pre-existing code templates/libraries is allowed, but the core solution must be built during the event
  6. Plagiarism in any form will lead to immediate disqualification
  7. Teams must adhere to the event timeline and submission deadlines
  8. Organizers' decisions will be final and binding

Code of Conduct

  1. Maintain professional and respectful behavior with all participants, organizers, and judges
  2. No discrimination, harassment, or inappropriate behavior will be tolerated
  3. Respect the venue facilities and equipment
  4. Keep workspaces clean and organized
  5. Follow all safety and security protocols during the overnight event
  6. Any violation of the code of conduct may result in disqualification

Technical Guidelines

  1. Teams must use their own laptops and equipment
  2. Power outlets and Wi-Fi will be provided
  3. Teams are responsible for backing up their own work
  4. Internet access will be available, but teams should prepare for potential connectivity issues
  5. Any external APIs, libraries, or frameworks can be used
  6. Website must be hosted on a publicly accessible platform (Vercel, Netlify, GitHub Pages, etc.)

Development Guidelines

  1. All development must happen during the designated event hours
  2. Teams can use online resources, documentation, and tutorials
  3. External mentorship or help from non-team members is not allowed during development
  4. AI tools (ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, etc.) can be used for assistance
  5. Teams must be prepared to explain their code and business logic during presentations

Presentation Guidelines

  1. All team members should be present during the presentation
  2. Presentation time limit will be strictly enforced
  3. Teams must demonstrate their working website during presentation
  4. Question and answer session will follow each presentation
  5. No changes to submissions allowed after the deadline

Accommodation and Facilities

  1. Overnight accommodation will be provided in designated classrooms
  2. Separate rest areas for male and female participants
  3. Meals (Dinner, Breakfast) will be provided
  4. Basic first aid facilities available
  5. Security personnel present throughout the event

Disqualification Criteria 

Teams will be disqualified for:

  • Plagiarism or copying others' work
  • Violation of code of conduct
  • Failure to meet submission deadlines
  • Presentation of pre-built solutions
  • Incomplete team presence
  • Misconduct or inappropriate behavior

events.title: Perfios Fintech Innovation Challenge
events.organizer: Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Bombay

raw.title: Perfios Fintech Innovation Challenge
raw.details:
Perfios presents the Fintech Innovation Challenge, in collaboration with E-Cell, IIT Bombay a premier hackathon and bootcamp series designed exclusively for IIT and IIM students passionate about building the future of fintech.

Why participate?

  • Compete for ₹2 lakh in cash prizes
  • Unlock support for incubation and future funding
  • Receive hands-on mentoring from industry experts
  • Gain official recognition certificates for all participants

What makes this challenge unique?

  • Intense focus on real-world fintech and insurtech applications
  • Exclusive access to data, APIs, and expert industry guidance
  • Multiple challenge themes:
    • Health Insurance (AI-driven preventive insurance)
    • Agri Lending & Insurance (satellite & agri-data based solutions)
    • Fraud Prevention (advanced behavioral/biometric analytics)

Key Details:

  • Eligibility: Open only to students from any IIT or IIM.
  • Team Rules: Inter-college and inter-specialization teams welcome.
  • Team Size: 2-4 members.
  • Venue: IIT Bombay Campus.
  • Cash Prize: ₹2 lakh total pool + certificates for all + mentoring bootcamp for winners.
  • Timeline: Multi-phase selection, including mentoring, solution refinement, and demo day.

What’s in it for you?

  • Pitch your solution to mentors and investors
  • Access real industry problem statements
  • Network with India’s top student innovators and fintech leaders
  • Accelerate your career with possible internships, funding, and national recognition

Step up to the Perfios Fintech Innovation Challenge and help shape the future of financial technology, designed for India’s top talent!

events.title: SharkTank CRCE
events.organizer: Fr. Conceicao Rodrigues College of Engineering (FRCRCE), Bandra

raw.title: SharkTank CRCE
raw.details:
Disclaimer:

  • This event is open to college students, excluding Fr. CRCE Bandra
  • A separate registration link will be shared for students from Fr. CRCE Bandra

About the Event & Why Participate:

  • Annual Shark Tank-style competition for innovation, entrepreneurship, and creative thinking among undergraduate students
  • Participants pitch their ideas to seasoned startup professionals, receive valuable feedback, and experience real-world negotiation dynamics in a simulated investment setting
  • Prize pool: ₹12,000+
  • Opportunity to network, gain recognition, and showcase creativity, business acumen, and strategic thinking

Guidelines:

  • Eligibility: Open to all undergraduate college students
  • Team Size: Minimum 2, Maximum 4 members
  • Venue: Fr. Conceicao Rodrigues College of Engineering, Bandra (W), Mumbai
  • Dates and Duration: 10th October (Final Round) | 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM
  • Registration Fee: ₹250 per team
  • Registration Deadline: 5th October

Online Shortlisting Process (October 8th):

  • All registered participants go through an online shortlisting round via Zoom or Google Meet
  • Teams submit a brief presentation or pitch summary
  • Top 10 teams qualify for the main event at the college
  • Every team receives a digital participation certificate
  • Note: Online round evaluates ideas only; no negotiations with judges occur at this stage

Offline Format & Process (Only Shortlisted Candidates):

  • Round 1 – Judge Valuation Round: 5–7 minute pitch; baseline valuation assigned by sharks, final and non-negotiable
  • Round 2 – Open Negotiation Round: 10-minute pitch per team, followed by 10-minute Q&A and negotiation; judges decide to accept, counter, or reject the offer.
  • Round 3 – Investment + Equity Ask FInale : Top 6 teams are invited; they negotiate final deals, and valuations can increase by up to 20% based on outcomes.

Rules:

  • Teams must report at the start of the event
  • Maximum 4 members per team
  • Valid ID Cards and confirmation receipts mandatory
  • Plagiarism of ideas leads to disqualification
  • Misbehavior results in disqualification
  • Sharks’ decisions are final and binding
  • Real investments/mentorships are separate from the simulated equity game
  • Dress Code: Strictly formal or product-themed attire
  • Presentation must be technically compatible with event equipment
  • Product prototypes are recommended

Judging Criteria:

  • Novelty & Creativity
  • Pitch and Team Communication
  • Business Knowledge
  • Business Model and Scalability
  • Appropriate Sales Projection and Costing
  • Negotiation Skills

organisation.name: Fr. Conceicao Rodrigues College of Engineering (FRCRCE), Bandra

events.title: IDEATION'25
events.organizer: SIES Graduate School of Technology, Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra

raw.title: IDEATION'25
raw.details:
About the event:

  • Date: Friday, September 19, 2025
  • Venue: SIES Graduate School of Technology, Nerul

Introduction:

  • Our aim is to give back to the society in ways more than one, and to solve most of the commonly faced issues with the help of technical ideas, which can then be taken up as first-hand projects.
  • Participants will be given a problem statement which is a community issue and you will be given time to fill the gaps that our NGO partners face to implement such ideas.
  • At the end of the session, you have to present your idea and the most appropriate, optimal, practical and unparalleled idea wins the competition.

Eligibility:

  • Open to all B.Tech students across Mumbai
  • Students from any specialization within B.Tech are welcome
  • Both inter-college and inter-specialization teams are allowed

Team Formation:

  • Team size: 3–4 members
  • Teams must nominate a team leader for communication and coordination

Registration Fees:

  • Group of 3: ₹400
  • Group of 4: ₹450

Competition Format:

  • Round 1: Problem Statement Selection & Brainstorming
    • At 10:30 AM, problem statements (focused on community and social issues) will be distributed
    • A maximum of two teams can select the same problem statement, though participants are encouraged to choose unique ones
    • Teams will have two hours for brainstorming and preparing their solutions in a structured PPT format
  • Round 2: Pitching Phase
    • Teams will present their solutions to the judges
    • Each team will have 7–10 minutes including Q&A to pitch their idea
    • Judging will be based on innovation, practicality, social impact, and presentation clarity

Event Timeline:

  • 8:30 AM – Registration of participants
  • 9:15 AM – Inauguration ceremony
  • 10:30 AM – Ideathon Session (Problem Statements + Brainstorming)
  • 12:30 PM – Lunch (provided for all participants)
  • 1:15 PM – Pitching Phase (Team Presentations)
  • 4:00 PM – Tea Break
  • 4:30 PM – Valedictory & Prize Distribution

What’s in it for you?:

  • Winners will be awarded cash prizes and goodies
  • A platform to present creative, socially impactful ideas
  • Opportunity to collaborate with students from diverse fields
  • Experience that strengthens innovation, problem-solving, and presentation skills
  • Recognition and networking opportunities with judges, faculty, and peers

Join the official WhatsApp group for updates, announcements, and queries: https://chat.whatsapp.com/JAzwURNtUm5LHOO0gXFvtC?mode=ems_wa_c

Note: If you are a participant, it is mandatory to join this group to ensure you don’t miss any important updates or instructions regarding the event.

Ideation’25 isn’t just a competition; it’s a chance to bring forward ideas that matter. Participants will not only gain exposure but also contribute to addressing real-world issues faced by our community.

events.title: Codeissance 2025
events.organizer: TSEC CodeStorm

raw.title: Codeissance 2025
raw.details:
Welcome to CODEISSANCE 2025, the annual flagship hackathon hosted by TSEC CodeStorm!
Back with bigger challenges and sharper ideas, this year’s edition will bring together some of the brightest minds for a 24-hour hackathon designed to spark creativity, innovation, and impactful solutions.

  • Venue: Thadomal Shahani Engineering College, Bandra
  • Date: 26th–27th September, 2025
  • Free Participation – No registration fees at any stage!

Team Guidelines

  • Team Size: 2–4 members
  • Open to all undergraduate students only
  • Inter-college teams allowed 
  • Inter-specialization teams allowed 
  • Each participant can be part of only one team

Selection Criteria:

  • Resume Screening
  • Team Lead Interview Call: Teams will have their team lead attend a quick interview call.

Hackathon Format & Timeline

Day 1: 26th September 2025 (Friday)

  • 8:00 AM: Offline reporting at TSEC Campus
  • 10:00 AM – 27th September 10:00 AM: 24-hour onsite coding round begins

Day 2: 27th September 2025 (Saturday)

  • 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM: Internal Judging Round
  • 2:00 PM onwards: Final Judging & Winner Announcements

Problem Statements

  • Multiple real-world problem statements will be provided across the domains.
  • Teams are free to choose one problem statement to work on.

Domains include:

  • Web/App Development
  • Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
  • Industry Domain

Rewards & Recognition

  • Total Prize Pool: ₹60,000+ 
  • Top-performing team in each domain will be awarded
  • Special prizes & goodies from sponsors and partners
  • Participation certificates for all every participant will walk away with recognition for their effort and contribution

Perks and Facilities

  • Free meals and refreshments provided throughout the hackathon
  • Dedicated workspace with Wi-Fi, charging points, and rest zones
  • Mentorship sessions from industry professionals and alumni to guide your project
  • Networking opportunities with experts, sponsors, and fellow innovators

CODEISSANCE 2025 is more than just a hackathon it’s a celebration of creativity, innovation, and collaboration. It’s your chance to learn, build, and connect with like-minded peers while solving challenges that matter. From brainstorming to prototyping, from mentorship to pitching your ideas, every moment at CODEISSANCE is designed to inspire you.

events.title: HerSpark Ideathon 2025
events.organizer: Thakur College of Engineering and Technology (TCET), Mumbai

raw.title: HerSpark Ideathon 2025
raw.details:
We are excited to invite you to Aether's HerSpark Ideathon 2025 (Only Females), a unique event designed to empower young women in STEM by harnessing the power of technology for social good. At HerSpark, we believe in the potential of technology to transform lives and create a better world. Whether you’re passionate about tackling societal challenges, innovating for underserved communities, or exploring the intersection of technology and social impact, HerSpark offers a platform to showcase your creativity and problem-solving skills.

Our themes,“Money skills for everyone: safer, smarter, more inclusive”and "Tools for online safety, digital literacy, safe communication, data privacy," challenge participants to use their technical expertise and creativity to develop innovative solutions that address critical social issues. This is your chance to make a meaningful impact and win exciting prizes.

Who can participate? 

  • Participation is open exclusively to female students enrolled in
    • Junior College and Diploma College from Science Streams
    • Undergraduate College from Science and Engineering Streams

Team Composition:

  • All members must be female.
  • Each team must consist of 2-4 members.
  • All team members must belong to the same educational category (e.g., Junior College/Diploma or Undergraduate/Engineering College).
  • Each team must nominate a Team Leader to serve as the primary point of contact.
  • Participants will work in teams to tackle challenges aligned with our two primary tracks

Why Participate?

  • Opportunity to Innovate: Create and develop solutions that can make a tangible impact on society.
  • Collaborate with Peers: Work with like-minded individuals passionate about technology and social issues.
  • Gain Recognition: Showcase your skills to industry experts and gain visibility in the tech community.
  • Exciting Prizes: Compete for trophies, gifts, and other exciting rewards.

Tracks:

Track 1: FinTech & Digital Literacy for Everyone

(Open to Junior College and Diploma College girl students from Commerce, Business, and Science Streams)

This track empowers young women to explore the intersection of finance and technology. Participants will delve into the world of FinTech, focusing on how digital tools and platforms can make financial services more accessible, inclusive, and secure. The goal is to equip students with the knowledge and skills to navigate digital finance safely, develop smarter money management practices, and foster financial literacy among underserved communities. By addressing the challenges of financial inclusion, this track encourages designing innovative solutions that make financial systems safer and more inclusive for everyone.

Track 2: Cyber Safety & Digital Empowerment

(Open to Undergraduate College girl students from Engineering, Computer Science, and Technology Streams)

This track challenges students to explore the essential tools and strategies for maintaining safety in the digital age. Participants will examine online safety protocols, data privacy, secure communication practices, and how to recognize and respond to cyber threats. The focus is on empowering women to become digital leaders in creating safe online environments, promoting digital literacy, and developing resources that encourage secure internet usage. By tackling issues like identity theft, online harassment, and data breaches, this track aims to prepare young women to advocate for and build safer digital spaces for all users.

Two Rounds:

  • Round 1 (Online): Teams will present their project ideas and materials to the panel
  • Round 2 (Offline):
    • Offline Round  Duration: 6 hours
    • Offline Round Venue: Thakur College of Engineering and Technology, Mumbai
    • Attendance is mandatory for all selected participants

We would love for you and your peers to join us for this exciting event. For more information please do go through the Guidelines PPT attached or you can also reach out to us at tcet.lr@tcetmumbai.in .

We look forward to seeing your ideas light up the path to a brighter future!

events.title: ENIGMA 4.0: A Web Development Hackathon
events.organizer: SIES Graduate School of Technology, Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra

raw.title: ENIGMA 4.0: A Web Development Hackathon
raw.details:
KINDLY NOTE: Since it's a one-day hackathon only, THERE IS NO ACCOMMODATION PROVIDED

THE PPT/ SUBMIT BUTTON will only be visible AFTER THE REGISTRATION FEES ARE PAID.

Eligibility

  • Open to all undergraduate students across India.
  • Students from any specialisation (Engineering, Management, Design, etc.) are welcome.
  • Both inter-college and inter-specialisation teams are allowed.

Team Formation

  • Team size: 3–4 members.
  • Teams must nominate a team leader for communication purposes.

Problem Statements

  • You can select any one problem statement of your choice for Round 1 and continue with it.
  • Problem statements for the hackathon (Round 2) may or may not be related to the problem statement you will choose for Round 1.
  • You will have to continue with the track you select in round 1 for the entire duration of the competition.
  • The problem statements for the OFFLINE ROUND will be provided on the day

COMPETITION FORMAT

2 Tracks: FinTech and HealthTech

Round 1: Idea Submission - PPT ROUND (Online)

  • The problem statements for Round 1 can be of your choice (but must be related to the track selected)
  • Teams must submit their proposed solution/approach in a structured PPT format.
  • The Submission for the PPT round has to be in the given PPT format strictly
  • Last date to submit: 07th September

Round 2: Prototype Development (Hackathon Round – Onsite at SIES Graduate School of Technology, Nerul)

  • Only 40 shortlisted teams will move to the OFFLINE ROUND and will build a working prototype/MVP.
  • You will have to continue with the track you select in round 1 for the entire duration of the competition.

  • Problem statements for the hackathon (Round 2) may or may not be related to the problem statement you will choose for Round 1.
  • Duration: Full Day (continuous hackathon format).
  • Mentorship sessions will be conducted during this round.

Round 3: Final Presentation & Pitch

  • TOP 10 Teams present their solution in front of judges and industry experts.
  • Evaluation based on innovation, feasibility, technical depth, and presentation.
  • Duration: 10 mins per team (including Q&A).

If not selected for the offline round, an amount of 300rs (3-member team) and 350rs (4-member team) will be refunded.

raw.details:
Cognition GameJam 2025 | SIES Graduate School of Technology:

  • Cognition GameJam 2025 is your chance to step into the exciting world of game development! This isn’t just about making a game — it’s about showing creativity, technical skills, and adaptability under real hackathon conditions. Whether you’re a solo developer or part of a team, this is your platform to shine.

  • GameJam is a fast-paced, multi-round hackathon where developers create innovative games under time and design constraints. With teams of 1-4 members, you’ll start by building a prototype that showcases your fundamental skills and then face the ultimate challenge in the final round when the theme is revealed!

Why Participate:

  • Learn from Experts: Access workshops and tutorials on game design, Unity, Unreal, and more.
  • Industry Exposure: Get your game reviewed by judges from the gaming industry.
  • Networking: Connect with game developers, designers, and enthusiasts.

  • Game Development Workshop : 7th September 2025
  • Prototype Submission: 14th September 2025
  • Theme Reveal: 19th September 2025
  • Finals & Showcase: 20th September 2025

*WhatsApp Group :

For further details regarding the event and workshop, join the WhatsApp group below:

Join Group!

Location:

  • Final Round: On-Campus

Registration Fee: Free
Eligibility: Open to all

Judging Criteria:

  • Prototype Round: Functionality, Gameplay Elements, Technical Implementation
  • Final Round: Creativity, Innovation, Execution, Adaptability to Twist

Additional Perks:

  • Hands-on Workshops on game engines and design principles

events.title: Bit N Build Around the World 2025
events.organizer: Fr. Conceicao Rodrigues College of Engineering (FRCRCE), Bandra

raw.title: Bit N Build Around the World 2025
raw.details:
Bit N Build is the flagship international hackathon conducted by the Google Developer Student Clubs of Fr. Conceicao Rodrigues College of Engineering, Mumbai in association with over 20+ states nationally and 20 countries internationally.

It is an event that has gained immense popularity and success in its foremost edition. From the last two years, this is the best hackathon conducted by Fr. Conceicao Rodrigues College of Engineering, Bandra, Mumbai, and has had 2500+ participants, 400+ teams and over 1.5 lakh site impressions.

For its newest edition, Bit N Build builds on its record of international success, having already made waves across the globe. This year, the hackathon sets its sights on expanding its national legacy through a two-phase format. In the first phase, participating colleges will conduct internal rounds across various states. The second phase will bring together the winning teams from each state for the ultimate face-off in Mumbai, where they will compete in high-stakes challenges designed to test their creativity, skill, and collaboration. The hackathon’s final round will feature the top teams from this phase, earning them an invitation to compete in Mumbai at Fr. Conceicao Rodrigues College of Engineering.

Rules and Guidelines:

Preliminary Round:

  • There will be Round 1 of Bit N Build open to all colleges across India.
  • Winners of Round 1 will qualify to compete in the final round of Bit N Build.

Eligibility:

  • The hackathon is open to all students pursuing bachelor's degrees. 
  • The participants must participate for the preliminary round in their respective states only.

Registration Fees:

  • A registration fee of Rs. 200 will be applicable per team.

Team Formation:

  • Individual participation is not allowed.
  • Teams can consist of 2 to 4 members.
  • Participants can form their own teams.
  • The team can have people from different colleges, but everyone must be from the same state.

Duration:

  • The hackathon will run for a span of 24 hours for the final round which will be held at Fr. Conceicao Rodrigues College of Engineering

Code of Conduct:

  • All participants must maintain a respectful and inclusive environment.
  • Any form of discrimination, harassment, or disrespectful behavior will not be tolerated.

Original Work:

  • All projects must be original and developed during the 24-hour hackathon.
  • The use of pre-existing projects or substantial pre-written code is not allowed, if found it may lead to immediate disqualification from the hackathon.

Submission Requirements:

  • Projects must be submitted before the specified 24-hour deadline.
  • Submissions should include a clear project description, demo, and any necessary documentation.
  • Participants are required to update their GitHub repositories and push code at least once every 3 hours during the hackathon.

Technology Stack:

  • Participants are free to use any programming languages, frameworks, and tools.
  • It is recommended to disclose the technology stack used in the project.

Judging Criteria:

  • Projects will be judged based on creativity, technical complexity, practicality, and overall presentation.
  • Judges' decisions are final.

General Instructions:

  • Shortlisted teams traveling for the grand finale will be eligible to receive reimbursement for to & from journey via 2nd class sleeper railway fare.
  • Meals will be provided to all the participants throughout the duration of the hackathon.

BitNBuild's initial round spans across multiple states and countries, challenging bright minds to innovate. The victorious contenders will converge in Mumbai, where the stage is set for the ultimate showdown and a chance to be crowned the hackathon champion!

Kindly check the registration deadline for your respective state and register for the Internal Hackathon before it starts for your respective state.

organisation.name: Fr. Conceicao Rodrigues College of Engineering (FRCRCE), Bandra

events.title: HackRobo 1.0
events.organizer: Pillai College Of Enginering

raw.title: HackRobo 1.0
raw.details:
HackRobo 1.0 is a power-packed, 2-day national-level hardware hackathon designed to bring together creative technologists, engineers, and innovators from across the country. Participants will be challenged to ideate, build, and showcase intelligent crawling robotic solutions that tackle real-world challenges in disaster relief, industrial maintenance, environmental monitoring, and more.

From hands-on prototyping to real-time simulations, HackRobo 1.0 encourages cross-disciplinary collaboration between robotics, IoT, embedded systems, and mechanical innovation — pushing participants to bring bold engineering ideas to life.

Payment:

Click here for payment
Once the payment is done, register and upload the payment receipt received via email (check your spam folder too). Our team will verify and contact the Team Leader for further details.

Date: 19–20 September 2025
Duration: 2 Days
Team Size: 3–5 Members
Registration Fee: ₹2000 per team
Mode: Offline
Venue: Pillai College of Engineering, New Panvel

Why HackRobo 1.0?

  • Real-World Hardware Innovation
  • Mentor Support and Guidance
  • Cross-Disciplinary Networking
  • Jamming and Collaboration Sessions
  • Exclusive Goodies for Girl Participants

  • Industrial Exposure and Hands-on Learning

Prize Pool: ₹50,000+

  • Top 3 Teams – Cash Prizes
  • Top 5 Teams – Extra Goodies
  • Winner and Participation Certificates
  • Internship Opportunities for high-performing teams

Theme: Intelligent Crawling Bot for Real-World Impact

Step into the future of robotics. Your mission is to design and prototype crawling robots capable of navigating difficult environments and performing critical functions across various sectors. Choose your challenge:

  • RescueBot – Search and rescue operations in disaster-hit areas
  • PipeGuard – Pipeline inspection in industrial setups
  • TerraQuest – Underground and tunnel exploration
  • AgriCrawler – Agricultural field monitoring and data collection
  • BridgeBot – Structural inspection of bridges

Whether you're solving problems in infrastructure, safety, agriculture, or emergency response, HackRobo 1.0 is your chance to make a meaningful impact using intelligent robotics.

For detailed problem statements, visit:
https://tech.alegria.co.in/hardware

Facilities:

  • Meals Included (Breakfast, Lunch, and Dinner for 2 Days)

Additional Perks:

  • Tech Talks and Fun Interactions

Rules and Guidelines:

  • Teams must consist of 3–5 members; cross-college teams are allowed
  • Projects must be original and developed during the hackathon
  • A working prototype or simulation is mandatory for the final round
  • Submissions must be made via Google Form
  • Judges' decisions are final and binding
  • Plagiarism or unethical behavior will lead to disqualification
  • Participants must stay on campus for the full duration of the event

Event Timeline:

    • Networking and Jamming
    • Dinner and Overnight Build
  • Day 2 – 20 September 2025

events.title: Incuverse 1.0
events.organizer: Somaiya Vidyavihar University

raw.title: Incuverse 1.0
raw.details:
Where Ideas Begin to Build
Incuverse 1.0 is a 24-hour innovation sprint crafted to give college innovators a real taste of turning ideas into ventures, with the right mix of creativity, collaboration, and practical problem-solving.

This isn’t just another hackathon, it’s a space where ideas take shape, skills are explored, and solutions are built with purpose. Participants receive real-world problem statements at registration, form diverse teams, and work across tech, design, and strategy to bring their solutions to life.

From presentations and prototypes to voice-over pitches, every element of the experience is designed to reflect what real-world building feels like, guided by expert mentors and supported by a growing community.

What to expect:

  • Problem statements that matter
  • A platform to explore your potential
  • Collaboration with creative minds across domains
  • An opportunity to be part of something that lasts beyond the event

The top teams will be invited to the on-ground Grand Finale to showcase their work, win exciting prizes, and get a chance for incubation support at riidl - a space where student-led ideas grow into real ventures.
Incuverse 1.0 is powered by riidl HACK, a student-led initiative under riidl that believes hackathons can be more than events, they can be launchpads for long-term impact.

PPT Template and Problem statements are listed on our official website : https://incuverse.vercel.app and https://riidl.org

events.title: IDEATHON: Spark Before the Code
events.organizer: Vasantdada Patil Pratishthan's College of Engineering and Visual Arts, Mumbai, Maharashtra

raw.title: IDEATHON: Spark Before the Code
raw.details:
Event Overview

Ideathon 2025 is a prestigious national-level innovation challenge hackathon organized by the Department of Information Technology in association with the Training and Placement Cell (TPO) of Vasantdada Patil Pratishthan's College of Engineering and Visual Arts (VPPCOE, Sion).
With the theme "Spark Before the Code", the event is focused on empowering the next generation of tech entrepreneurs and problem solvers across India.

Purpose and Vision

  • Designed to ignite creativity, entrepreneurial thinking, and innovation among engineering students.
  • Aims to bridge the gap between academia and industry by addressing real-world challenges with scalable tech solutions.
  • Promotes community impact and interdisciplinary collaboration.

Who Can Participate

  • Open to all engineering students across India, regardless of specialization or year of study.
  • Team Size: 2 to 4 members per team.

Registration and Submission Process

  • Participants must register on Unstop.
  • Submit a presentation (PPT) outlining innovative solutions to domain-specific or local impact problems.
  • A wide range of problem statements : Link to Problem Statements

Evaluation Criteria for Initial Round

  • Submissions will be evaluated by academic and industry mentors on:
    • Clarity of idea
    • Feasibility
    • Innovation
    • Societal impact
    • Technical approach

Offline Pitching Round

  • Top 25 to 30 teams will be shortlisted for an offline pitching round at the VPPCOE campus.
  • Simulates an investor pitch scenario before startup founders, domain experts, and industry mentors.
  • Five parallel judging panels will evaluate based on:
    • Innovation
    • Societal relevance
    • Scalability
    • Execution strategy

Rewards and Recognition

  • Cash prizes, certificates, and direct qualification to Quasar 4.0 (VPPCOE’s flagship national hackathon in March 2026).
  • Participants also benefit from:
    • Expert feedback
    • Networking opportunities
    • Practical experience in pitching and defending startup ideas

What Makes Ideathon 2025 Unique

  • Strong emphasis on innovation with purpose.
  • Focus on problem-solving with measurable outcomes.
  • Encourages:
    • Entrepreneurial mindset
    • User-centric design thinking
    • Early-stage startup development skills

Why You Should Participate

  • First step toward turning ideas into startup-ready concepts.
  • A platform to showcase innovation, build leadership skills, and connect with experts.
  • Celebrate student-led innovation and fuel your journey into tech entrepreneurship.

Join Us: Official WhatsApp Community  IDEATHON | Updates & Announcements

  • If you're ready to decode the start-up equation and solve real-world problems through innovation, Ideathon 2025 is your platform.

raw.title: Launchpad: Vibe Coding SaaS Startup Challenge
raw.details:
Where creativity meets cutting-edge AI and product thinking.

Ready to move beyond solving problems and start creating solutions? This challenge is designed for aspiring innovators who want to build real-world applications using the latest in AI tools and SaaS frameworks. Whether you’re a design thinker, a code wizard, or an AI enthusiast this is your launchpad to the future.

About the Contest:

Launchpad is a two-round, team-based innovation competition that tasks participants with building AI-powered SaaS applications. From idea to prototype, teams will brainstorm impactful solutions, engineer smart AI features using prompt engineering, and pitch their final products in front of an expert jury.

  • Round 1 – Ideation & Prototyping: Teams will code on campus during the contest hours.

  • Round 2 – Pitching: Only selected finalists will present their ideas and prototypes to the judging panel.

     Duration: 2 Days
     Tools: Generative AI APIs, No-code/Low-code platforms, Prompt Engineering Tools
     Mode: Offline

Eligibility Criteria:

  • Open to all undergraduate students currently enrolled in any college
  • Teams must consist of 2 to 4 members
  • Cross-college and cross-domain collaboration is welcome!

Rules & Guidelines:

  • Each team must register in advance.
  • Use of AI tools must be clearly documented in the submission
  • Plagiarism, idea theft, or code sharing across teams is strictly prohibited
  • Final decisions rest with the organizing committee and judging panel

Perks & Prizes:

Top 3 Teams

  • Cash Prizes 
  • Certificate of Excellence 

Join Whatsapp group for updates: LaunchPad

Note: NO ACCOMODATION WILL BE PROVIDED BY US.

Inspired by the legendary ICPC format but with an exciting twist, this contest is built to challenge your speed, logic, and precision. Whether you're a beginner looking to test your DSA skills or a seasoned coder aiming to stand out, this is your arena!

About the Contest:

Big-O Battle is a one-round individual competition conducted on the GeeksforGeeks platform. Participants will be presented with five DSA problems carefully curated to assess their algorithmic understanding and implementation skills.

The contest duration will be 2 hours, and submissions will be evaluated in real-time via the platform’s leaderboard.

Eligibility Criteria:

  • Open to all currently enrolled students (undergraduates).
  • Participants from any college or specialization are welcome.
  • This is a solo competition; team participation is not allowed.

Rules & Guidelines:

  • Each participant must register individually and compete alone.
  • Plagiarism, code sharing, or using AI assistance is strictly prohibited and may lead to disqualification.
  • Contest access links will be shared before the event.

events.title: Global Digital Health Summit Hackathon 2025
events.organizer: Global Digital Health Summit Expo & Innovation Awards

raw.title: Global Digital Health Summit Hackathon 2025
raw.details:
The Global Digital Health Summit Hackathon 2025 is your launchpad to innovate, build, and showcase your skills on a global stage—live at the world’s largest digital health summit.

Event Details:

Dates: 20–21 September 2025
Duration: 24-Hour Onsite Hackathon
Venue: Jio World Convention Centre, Mumbai
Team Size: 3–4 participants
Eligibility:

  • Open to students and recent graduates aged 18–23 years
  • Inter-college and inter-specialization teams allowed

What You’ll Build:

You’ll choose from predefined, real-world problem statements in digital health, including:

  • Multi-Agent Workflow for Patient Diagnostics: Design a system where different AI agents work together to help make better diagnoses. Each agent can take on a task like searching medical literature, scanning online health databases, reviewing past cases, or analyzing symptoms.
  • Chronic Disease Tracking & Comorbidity Prevention: Create a system to help people with chronic illnesses (like diabetes, asthma, or hypertension) track their condition daily and avoid related health problems (called comorbidities). The system can use data and trends to predict risks and suggest early actions.
  • Mental Health Monitoring with Gamification & AI: Build an AI-powered platform that helps users keep track of their mental health in a fun and engaging way. It could include mood tracking, small challenges, games, and personalized feedback, while also identifying serious signs like anxiety, depression, or suicidal thoughts early.

Additional challenges will be revealed as the summit approaches.

What’s in it for You:

  • Prize Pool worth INR 1,00,000
  • Certificates from the Dynamic Coalition on Gaming for Purpose and the Dynamic Coalition on Digital Health, affiliated with the United Nations Internet Governance Forum (UN-IGF)
  • On-site recruitment opportunities by leading healthcare and tech companies
  • Opportunities to network and interact with professionals from healthcare, policy, and innovation sectors
  • Each participant receives Delegate Passes for both this year and next year’s Summit, giving you free access to the conference experience beyond the hackathon
  • All meals and hacking resources provided at the venue

organisation.name: Global Digital Health Summit Expo & Innovation Awards

address_with_country_logo.address: Jio World Centre, G Block, Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra East

raw.title: HackCelestial 2.0
raw.details:
• Note - You will receive the payment link only if you are shortlisted.

HackCelestial 2.0

HackCelestial is a high-energy, 36-hour innovation sprint that brings together talented student developers, designers, and problem-solvers to tackle real-world challenges. Participants are challenged to design and develop impactful solutions using Generative AI and core software development skills. Through intense collaboration, rapid prototyping, and creative thinking, teams compete to solve specific problem statements while pushing the boundaries of technology and innovation. Whether you're here to learn, build, or lead, HackCelestial offers the perfect platform to code, collaborate, and create something extraordinary.

Date: 19-20 September 2025

No. of Days: 2 Days

Members Count : 3-5 per team 

Registration Fees: : Rs. 1500 per team (For Final Round)

Mode: Offline

Why HackCelestial 2.0

  • Real World Problem Solving
  • Networking Opportunities
  • Mentor Guidance
  • Jamming Sessions
  • Exciting Goodies
  • Internship Opportunities
  • Exclusive Goodies for Girl Participants

Prize Pool: Rs. 1,00,000 

Top 3 teams will receive cash prize

The top 5 teams will receive additional goodies as a special reward.

Award for Excellence in AI Application

Award for Excellence in User Interface Design

 

Theme: Technology for Social Impact

Calling all innovators, dreamers, and change-makers! This hackathon is your chance to harness the power of technology to tackle some of society’s biggest challenges. Whether it’s boosting access to education and healthcare, driving environmental sustainability, or sparking vibrant community connections, the possibilities to make a real impact are endless.

If you’re passionate about social justice, green initiatives, or economic growth, this is your moment to shine. We invite you to bring your creativity, critical thinking, and ethical approach to the table as we build solutions that uplift lives, strengthen communities, and protect our planet.

Your challenge, should you choose to accept it, is to develop solutions that create meaningful impact across these vital areas:

Health & Well-being: From expanding access to healthcare and predicting diseases to supporting mental health and advancing personalized medicine — let’s make well-being a reality for all.

Education & Learning: Craft tools that tailor learning to individual needs, break down accessibility barriers, boost literacy, or supercharge skill development.

Environment & Sustainability: Tackle climate change, conserve precious resources, monitor pollution, or enhance disaster prediction and response — because our planet deserves the best.

Inclusion & Equity: Work to bridge gaps, champion accessibility for all abilities, ensure fair resource distribution, and stand strong against discrimination.

Community & Safety: Build smart city innovations, enhance public safety, reduce crime, and bring communities closer together.

Humanitarian Aid: Innovate ways to support disaster relief, assist refugees, and optimize aid delivery when it’s needed most.

For sample problem statements check out our website https://tech.alegria.co.in/software

Facilities

Free Accommodation ( Accommodation within the campus)

Food Accessibility (Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner) 

Additional Perks

  • Participation Certificate
  • Winner Certificate
  • Internship Opportunities
  • Goodies

Rules & Guidelines:

Teams must consist of 3 to 5 members; cross-college teams are allowed.

Each team may submit only one idea, which must be original and developed during the hackathon.

Plagiarism or replication of existing solutions without significant modification will lead to immediate disqualification.

Submissions must be made through Unstop or Google Form, and all deadlines must be strictly followed; late submissions will not be evaluated.

A working prototype or simulation is mandatory Finals.

Judges’ decisions are final and binding, and they may request technical clarifications or design justifications during evaluations.

Prize distribution and internship opportunities for top-performing teams are subject to performance and interview rounds.

Teams must stay active and responsive regarding review schedules and shortlisting announcements.

All team members must bring their own devices and stay on campus for the full duration of the offline event; accommodation will be provided.

Participants are expected to maintain professionalism and discipline during all rounds and interactions.

Only official communications shared via the event website and email should be followed.

Teams violating rules or engaging in unethical practices will be disqualified immediately.

Arambh 2025: Innovate, Ideate & Implement

The Unit of Social Innovation & Entrepreneurship of the College of Social Work (Empowered Autonomous), Nirmala Niketan, Mumbai, is pleased to announce Arambh 2025, a dynamic platform dedicated to nurturing innovation, entrepreneurship, and youth-led problem-solving.

This year’s events include:

Idea Hackathon – Encouraging creative solutions to pressing social challenges.

Business Pitch – Showcasing innovative ideas, with a focus on both social impact and profit generation.

Date: 17th September 2025, Wednesday
Venue: College of Social Work (Empowered Autonomous), Nirmala Niketan, Goregaon Campus,  Goregaon (East), Mumbai – 400 063

Rules : 

  1. Any student registered for an undergraduate or postgraduate program.
  2. Both individual submissions and group submissions are eligible.
  3. Students may form a team of up to three students.
  4. One student/team could submit only one entry.
  5. Final authority for settling any disputes arising out of the competition shall be the jury of eminent scholars appointed by the organisers.
  6. Participants are responsible for covering their own travel expenses to and from the event venue(No TA/DA will be provided by the Organizers)

Judging Criteria : 

  •  Innovation (25%)
  • Scalability of solution (25%)
  •  Ability to articulate an idea based on evidence (25%
  •  Interdisciplinary (25%)

We invite academic institutions, NGOs, corporates, and individuals to explore partnerships and sponsorship opportunities to support this initiative. Join us in fostering India’s entrepreneurial spirit for a more resilient and equitable future. Reach out to: cswnn.masie@gmail.com

raw.title: Need For Code 4.0
raw.details:
Organized by TSEC CodeTantra, Thadomal Shahani Engineering College, Mumbai

Welcome to Need For Code 4.0, the flagship annual hackathon hosted by TSEC CodeTantra! Back with an all-new retro gaming theme, this year’s edition promises more excitement, more innovation, and now, more hours — with a 48-hour hackathon format that lets you code, compete, and collaborate at your very best.

This event is your chance to take on real-world problem statements and build tech-driven solutions with the potential to make lasting impact — across domains like Web Development, AI/ML, Blockchain, and Social Cause. Whether you’re a beginner or an advanced coder, this is where ideas turn into execution.

Venue : Thadomal Shahani Engineering College,Bandra.

Date : 5th-7th August, 2025

Note: Registration fee of Rs.500 will be applicable only to the teams shortlisted for the offline hackathon round starting on 5th August.

Team Guidelines

  • Team Size: 2-4 members.
  • Open to all students from any college or specialization.
  • Inter-college and inter-specialization teams allowed.

Hackathon Format & Timeline :

Day 1: 5th August 2025 (Tuesday)

  • 9:00 AM: Offline reporting at TSEC Campus
  • 11:00 AM – 6th August 11:00 AM: 24-hour onsite coding round begins

Day 2: 6th August 2025 (Wednesday)

  • 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM: Internal Judging Round
  • Participants return home after internal judging
  • 9:00 PM: Final-Shortlisted teams announced for final judging round

Day 3: 7th August 2025 (Thursday)

  • 10:00 AM: Finalists report back to TSEC.
  • 11:00 AM onwards: Final Judging & Winner Announcements

Problem Statements :

  • Multiple real-world problem statements will be provided.
  • Teams are free to choose one problem statement to build their solution around.
  • Domains include: Web/App Development, AI/ML, Blockchain, and Social Cause.
  • Problem statements will be released on 4th August at midnight.

Rewards & Recognition : 

Total Prize Pool: ₹80,000

₹20,000 awarded to the top-performing team in each domain:

  • Web/App Development
  • AI/ML
  • Blockchain
  • Social Cause

Perks and Facilities :

  • All meals and refreshments are on us!
  • Dedicated workspace with internet access, rest areas, and a fun retro-themed setup.
  • Participation certificates, swag, networking opportunities, and sponsor interaction.

Rules & Regulations :

  • Plagiarism or reuse of previously published code is strictly prohibited. Projects must be original.
  • All team members must be present at the time of check-in on 5th August at 9:00 AM.
  • Teams should regularly push code to GitHub, as repositories may be reviewed during the event.
  • Discrimination of any kind will not be tolerated — all participants are expected to maintain a respectful environment.
  • Participants must follow the event schedule strictly.
  • Any damage to college property will result in penalties.
  • Suspicious activity or guideline violations should be reported to the organizing team immediately.

events.title: The Matrix Protocol - AI Hackathon
events.organizer: LenDenClub

raw.title: The Matrix Protocol - AI Hackathon
raw.details:
About our company:

LenDenClub Platforms is a Fintech powerhouse enabling credit access for millions across India through three distinct brands. LenDenClub, our RBI-registered NBFC-P2P platform, connects lenders and borrowers directly. InstaMoney, our Loan Service Provider, delivers instant loans across every pincode—serving credit-invisible segments. LenDenClub Services, our Technology Services arm, facilitates digital lending infrastructure between regulated Entities and Loan Service Providers Together, we’ve disbursed over ₹16,000 crore, empowered 2M+ users, and built an inclusive financial ecosystem that’s reshaping India’s credit landscape.

Join one of India’s fastest-growing Fintech & AI platform in a high-stakes hackathon that goes beyond code. From decoding borrower behavior to automating smart conversations, here’s your chance to solve real problems that shape how millions access credit.

Think beyond the usual. Build intelligent systems that can transform finance landscape. 

If you're ready to create AI that truly makes a difference, this is it.

Why Join the Hackathon?

  • Fast-track your growth with real mentorship
    • Work closely with experts from the world of fintech and AI. Get hands-on guidance and honest feedback that helps you grow, not just as a coder, but as a problem-solver.
  • Build AI that truly makes a difference
    • Use your skills to create ethical, inclusive, and transparent solutions that can improve how people access and use financial services.
  • Bring tech closer to real-world finance
    • Dive into real challenges from the banking and lending world. Learn how to turn smart ideas into solutions that actually work for real people.
  • Team up, think big, make it count
    • Collaborate with driven innovators. Push your limits, share ideas, and be part of something that could reshape the future of finance for millions.

Eligibility & Team Rules:

  • Working professionals in Tech, AI, Finance, or Product roles
  • Students (UG/PG/PhD) from Applied Data Science or any relevant specialization
  • Freelancers & Startup founders with a passion for innovation
  • Team of up to 4 members — cross-functional teams encouraged
  • One person cannot be a member of more than one team
  • Any deviation from the above will result in immediate disqualification of the entire team

organisation.name: LenDenClub

address_with_country_logo.address: LenDenClub House, Mumbai
